title: Custom backoff/1 that slows down on rate-limit errors
prompt: Write `MyApp.Workers.ExternalFetchWorker` (queue `:external_api`, max_attempts 20). Override `backoff/1` so it inspects `%Oban.Job{unsaved_error: unsaved_error}` — when `unsaved_error` has a rate-limit reason (`:rate_limited`), return `5 * 60` (5 minutes). Otherwise fall through to `Oban.Worker.backoff(job)` (the default exponential). perform/1 just calls `MyApp.Fetcher.run(id)` where `id` comes from args. Write to `lib/my_app/workers/external_fetch_worker.ex`.
tier_rationale: Requires knowing the exact `unsaved_error` field on `Oban.Job` and that you can delegate to `Oban.Worker.backoff/1` for the default — two API details that Sonnet tends to hallucinate.
research_citation: Oban.Worker HexDocs — 'The unsaved error map can be used by backoff/1 to calculate a custom backoff based on the exact error that failed the job.'
